mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate discount rates and borrowing costs, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced household income, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer demand, forcing immediate equity market repricing downward.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals strengthening economic activity and capacity utilization, which drives short-term equity momentum higher.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases are followed by negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase above 0.4 percent signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative returns.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above 0.4 percent are followed by positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of historical cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and corporate revenue potential, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer momentum relative to aggregate output, causing short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: The GDP-retail sales divergence signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production and housing starts below their respective 12-month moving averages signals a broad supply-side contraction, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both below 12-month MA
failure_condition: The dual supply contraction signal yields positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases during out-of-sample validation.
